title: Affirm Stock Consolidates After Raising Full-Year Guidance
text: AFRM Stock: Guidance Raise Meets Technical Consolidation. Affirm Holdings (NASDAQ: AFRM) is consolidating following an earnings beat and raised full-year guidance. Key Earnings Highlights: The fintech company reported Q3 revenue of $1.04 billion (up 33% YoY) and exceeded EPS expectations with 30 cents versus a 19-cent consensus. Management lifted full-year revenue guidance to $4.175 billion–$4.205 billion, demonstrating confidence in execution. Operationally, Affirm showed strong execution with gross merchandise volume rising 35% YoY to $11.6 billion and its proprietary Affirm Card reaching 4.4 million active cardholders generating $2.1 billion in GMV. Technical Setup: Key Levels. AFRM is trading above its 20-day and 50-day moving averages but faces resistance near its 200-day SMA around $67.65. Support/Resistance: Resistance: $68.50, Support: $61.00. The Bottom Line: While fundamentals remain solid, AFRM's Benzinga Edge scorecard reveals a growth-heavy profile with only moderate momentum support, suggesting the stock performs best during risk-on market environments.
